markdownRemark.html:
What we will make?

We’re gonna create a Game Management List! You can store all your favorite games in one list for easy reference, you can review them yourself, give it scores, comments and the console you used! It will consist of a table connected to a toolbar that will have a searchbar to filter the games and a button which brings a modal with a form inside that will let us add games to the table. To delete it we will add on each row a checkbox input that will change the button to bring a delete form instead.

You might be wondering, don’t we need to create a backend server for all this? For now we will focus with Frontend development and our job is to make use of the information provided by the Backend, so instead of a traditional server, we will use a tool called JSON Server, which will let us simulate a backend server in a very simple way!

This is important because we want to be using Redux Thunk on this project for making async calls.

Lets get started!

Creating our enviroment.

We will use Create React App for this project:

npx create-react-app react-redux-games-list-management
cd react-redux-games-list-management
npm start

We erase all the files inside src folder until it looks like this:

  • src

    • App.js
    • index.js
    • serviceWorker.js

Now lets install redux and axios for async!

yarn add redux react-redux axios

Now lets build our folder’s structure, since its a small project, we will keep it simple for now.

  • src

    • actions

    • components

      • button
      • modal
      • searchbar
      • table
      • toolbar
    • pages
    • reducers
    • store
    • types
    • App.js
    • index.js

For store we will rewrite it like this:

From Redux we get createStore which is self explanatory, it takes the rootReducer, the initial state, compose which is let us combine functions and applyMiddleware which we use in order to apply Thunk in our app.

Inside index.js we will put the following code:

!code

Provider let us have the global state available for the whole app in this case. We could’ve put all the logic in the folder store inside index.js, but its cleaner to have everything in its rightful place, otherwise it can get messy.

For now this is enough setup to get started with our project in the next section.

Lets create our service!

We’re going to use a tool called JSON Server which will let us create our service which we will use for CRUD (create, read, update, delete) purposes. We will start by installing it:

npm install -g json-server

Now create a file called db.json and put the following data for now:

Start the server with json-server —watch db.json.

Now if we go to http://localhost:3000/games we will see our service right there! Notice that if we change the route to http://localhost:3000/games/1 we will get the first object.

Starting the project!

We be building a game list management system

Conclusion

Eng Adrian Beria.
